(also known by various Syriac [transliterations](/wiki/Transliteration "Transliteration") such as **Kaitha** or **Qaita**) is a [liturgical season](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year") in the [East Syriac Rite](/wiki/East_Syriac_Rite "East Syriac Rite") of Christianity. The season begins on the seventh Sunday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") (just after the conclusion of the [Season of Apostles](/wiki/Season_of_Apostles "Season of Apostles")) and continues for seven weeks. The Season of Summer has a theme of spiritual harvest, reflecting on the fruits of the missionary labors of the [Apostles](/wiki/Apostles_in_the_New_Testament "Apostles in the New Testament").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

de Souza, Rev. Raymond (July 29, 2020). ["Syro-Malabar calendar elevates summer feasts"](https://www.catholicregister.org/opinion/columnists/item/31921-fr-raymond-de-souza-syro-malabar-calendar-elevates-summer-feasts). *[The Catholic Register](/wiki/The_Catholic_Register "The Catholic Register")*.

Madhavathu, Maryann (2016). [*Liturgy as a Sacrament of the Paschal Mystery: A Study of the Theological Relations between the Liturgy of the Eucharist and the Liturgy of the Hours in the Roman and Syro-Malabar Rites*](https://core.ac.uk/download/pdf/80792869.pdf) (PDF) (Thesis). [Katholieke Universiteit Leuven](/wiki/KU_Leuven "KU Leuven"). p. 7.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about the East Syriac liturgical season. For the meteorological season, see [Summer](/wiki/Summer "Summer").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/Qaita.jpg/250px-Qaita.jpg)](/wiki/File:Qaita.jpg)

An imaginative depiction of [Jesus](/wiki/Jesus "Jesus") and an angel as harvesters.

| [Liturgical seasons](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year") |
| --- |
| * Pre-Christmas   + [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent") (*Western*)   + [Nativity Fast](/wiki/Nativity_Fast "Nativity Fast") (*Byzantine*)   + [Annunciation](/wiki/Season_of_Annunciation "Season of Annunciation") (*Syriac*) * [Christmas](/wiki/Christmastide "Christmastide") * [Epiphany](/wiki/Epiphany_season "Epiphany season")   + [Ordinary Time](/wiki/Ordinary_Time "Ordinary Time") (*Western*) * [Pre-Lent](/wiki/Pre-Lent "Pre-Lent") * [Lent](/wiki/Lent "Lent") (*Western*) / [Great Lent](/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent") (*Eastern*) * [Paschal Triduum](/wiki/Paschal_Triduum "Paschal Triduum") (*Western*) * [Easter](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide") * [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost_season "Pentecost season")   + [Ordinary Time](/wiki/Ordinary_Time "Ordinary Time") (*Western*) * Summer (*East Syriac*) * [Apostles' Fast](/wiki/Apostles%27_Fast "Apostles' Fast") (*Eastern*) * [Dormition Fast](/wiki/Dormition_Fast "Dormition Fast") (*Eastern*) * [Elijah–Cross–Moses](/w/index.php?title=Elijah%E2%80%93Cross%E2%80%93Moses&action=edit&redlink=1 "Elijah–Cross–Moses (page does not exist)") (*East Syriac*) * [Dedication of the Church](/w/index.php?title=Season_of_the_Dedication_of_the_Church&action=edit&redlink=1 "Season of the Dedication of the Church (page does not exist)") (*Syriac*) |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Liturgical_year "Template:Liturgical year") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year "Template talk:Liturgical year") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Liturgical_year "Special:EditPage/Template:Liturgical year") |

The **Season of Summer** (also known by various Syriac [transliterations](/wiki/Transliteration "Transliteration") such as **Kaitha** or **Qaita**) is a [liturgical season](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year") in the [East Syriac Rite](/wiki/East_Syriac_Rite "East Syriac Rite") of Christianity. The season begins on the seventh Sunday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") (just after the conclusion of the [Season of Apostles](/wiki/Season_of_Apostles "Season of Apostles")) and continues for seven weeks. The Season of Summer has a theme of spiritual harvest, reflecting on the fruits of the missionary labors of the [Apostles](/wiki/Apostles_in_the_New_Testament "Apostles in the New Testament").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Fridays of Summer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Season_of_Summer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Fridays of Summer")]

The Fridays of various weeks of this liturgical season are dedicated to various martyrs.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

* First Friday of Summer: St. [James of Nisibis](/wiki/Jacob_of_Nisibis "Jacob of Nisibis")
* Second Friday of Summer: St. [Mari](/wiki/Saint_Mari "Saint Mari")
* Fifth Friday of Summer: St. [Simoni and seven sons](/wiki/Woman_with_seven_sons "Woman with seven sons")
* Sixth Sunday of Summer: St. [Simon Barsaba and co-martyrs](/wiki/Shemon_Bar_Sabbae "Shemon Bar Sabbae")

["Syro-Malabar calendar elevates summer feasts"](https://www.catholicregister.org/opinion/columnists/item/31921-fr-raymond-de-souza-syro-malabar-calendar-elevates-summer-feasts). *[The Catholic Register](/wiki/The_Catholic_Register "The Catholic Register")*.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Madhavathu, Maryann (2016). [*Liturgy as a Sacrament of the Paschal Mystery: A Study of the Theological Relations between the Liturgy of the Eucharist and the Liturgy of the Hours in the Roman and Syro-Malabar Rites*](https://core.ac.uk/download/pdf/80792869.pdf) (PDF) (Thesis). [Katholieke Universiteit Leuven](/wiki/KU_Leuven "KU Leuven"). p. 7.
